title: [v4.0] slirp: fix setup on ipv6 disabled systems
body:
This is an automated cherry-pick of #13485

/assign baude

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *openshift-cherrypick-robot* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.0/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Nice, is it also possible when I have a PR with several commit to only cherry pick a specific one?

> Nice, is it also possible when I have a PR with several commit to only cherry pick a specific one?

i frankly don't know.  Seems like something we should try?
also -> https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/abayer/test-infra/prow/external-plugins/cherrypicker#section-documentation  the part about doing it before merge is awesome
Looks like some kind of Make issue on this branch?  @cevich?

Changes LGTM. 
Another to DO NOT MERGE until we get an exception.

This is the 4.0 branch, shouldn't this be used for upstream use only?
I thought RHEL uses 4.0-rhel, am I misunderstanding something?

This PR needs to be rebased to fix up the windows test.
@baude You know the rebase command?
@mheon had wanted to keep the two in sync for a bit @Luap99
Ya, this should be fixed by my backport PR (that just merged today).  `git remote update && git rebase -i upstream/v4.0` and force-push should take care of it.
@Luap99 I'm just going to manually cherry-pick this
